metrics.label: Vitalik's main wallet (vitalik.eth)

verdict.headline: Safe to transact — wallet is publicly doxxed as vitalik.eth and passes all sanctions checks.
verdict.reasoning: Both sanctions sources (Chainalysis on-chain oracle and the x402 sanctions service) returned clean results, satisfying the most important screening requirement. The wallet resolves via ENS to vitalik.eth, a strong public identity attribution comparable to a known-safe label. On-chain history shows a healthy non-zero balance (~5.68 ETH) on a well-known long-lived address, providing additional positive supporting evidence. Web sentiment was unresolved and contract analysis is not applicable (EOA), but these do not undermine the strong positives.

findings.finding: Chainalysis on-chain oracle reports isSanctioned=false and x402 sanctions service returned no matches — wallet is sanctions-clean.
ENS reverse lookup resolves to vitalik.eth — wallet is publicly doxxed to a well-known identity.
Neither the x402 labeler nor the eth-labels registry returned any negative attributions; no scam/mixer/exploit tags present.
Address holds ~5.68 ETH balance on a long-established, widely-known wallet — consistent with legitimate activity.
Web sentiment search was unresolved; no negative press surfaced (treated as neutral).

services.rationale: All candidates have unknown failure rate and quality; resource 0 (anchor-x402) ties resource 1 on price ($0.001) and completeness (2/3) but uses a more purpose-built dedicated domain, and both beat all other candidates on price and completeness — resource 0 is preferred as the cleaner named endpoint over the raw AWS API Gateway URL of resource 1.

All candidates share unknown failure rate and quality; resource 1 has a description that directly matches label/entity classification ('exchange, mixer, contract or known entity') at the lowest non-cluster price ($0.005), and completeness is equal across most options — the entity-attribution hint on the cluster candidates (2/3/5) is a soft signal that doesn't override the clearer labeling intent of resource 1.

Resource 1 is the only candidate tied for lowest price ($0.0039) while also achieving inputInfoCompleteness 2/3 (method + params documented), making it the strongest choice when all failure rates and quality scores are unknown.

No candidate explicitly describes web/social sentiment, so selecting the best-fit risk/reputation option; resource 1 is chosen over resource 0 (same underlying API) because its 2/3 completeness score better documents the input shape, reducing call-failure risk.
